concept.name: implicit grid row creation
concept.explain:
The 'grid-row' property can be used to place an item in a row that doesn't exist yet.
Grid will automatically create an implicit row to accommodate the item.

ui.prompt: Move the car to the highlighted bay on row 8. Use an implicit row while keeping it in column 2.

entities.baseCss: background: rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.22); outline: 2px dashed rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.8); border-radius: 10px;

concept.name: implicit grid column creation
concept.explain:
The 'grid-column' property can be used to place an item in a column that doesn't exist yet.
Grid will automatically create an implicit column to accommodate the item.

ui.prompt: Move the car to the highlighted bay on column 8. Keep it on row 4.

entities.baseCss: background: rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.22); outline: 2px dashed rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.8); border-radius: 10px;

concept.name: explicit vs implicit tracks
concept.explain:
When you place an item in an implicit row or column, that track will automatically expand to fit the item.
However, if you define an explicit track that overlaps with the implicit one, the explicit track will take precedence and the item will be sized to fit it instead.

ui.prompt: Park in the highlighted bay at row 8 / column 8, then define an explicit 8×8 template on .grid.
hints: Then declare explicit tracks, try: grid-template-columns: repeat(8, 60px); grid-template-rows: repeat(8, 60px);

entities.baseCss: background: rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.22); outline: 2px dashed rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.8); border-radius: 10px;
